conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I'll help you determine eligibility for Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

Let me start by asking some questions about each child.

First, can you please tell me the ages of Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 7 years old, Blake is 20, and Charlie is also 7 - they're twins.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

Now, regarding Blake who is 20 years old - is Blake currently in approved education or training? For example, is Blake enrolled in full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent) or an approved training program?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. Actually, Blake works full-time now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that about Blake.

Now I need to understand your living arrangements. Do all three children (Alex, Blake, and Charlie) currently live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, all three of them live with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask about your responsibility for the children.

Are you the parent or main person responsible for raising Alex, Blake, and Charlie? And do you provide for their care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm their parent and I'm responsible for raising them and looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. A few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you currently living in the UK, and do Alex, Blake, and Charlie also live in the UK with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we all live in the UK - we're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to ensure I have all the information needed.

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your children.

Let me provide you with the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility:**

✅ **Alex (age 7)** - You ARE eligible to claim Child Benefit
✅ **Charlie (age 7)** - You ARE eligible to claim Child Benefit
❌ **Blake (age 20)** - You are NOT eligible to claim Child Benefit

**Explanation:**

- **Alex and Charlie**: Both are 7 years old (under 16), live with you in the UK, you're their parent and responsible for them, and no one else is claiming for them. They both meet all the criteria for Child Benefit.

- **Blake**: At 20 years old and not in approved education or training, Blake doesn't qualify. Child Benefit can only be claimed for young people over 16 if they're under 20 AND in approved full-time non-advanced education or approved training. Since Blake is working full-time and not in education or training, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake.

You can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit claim?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 7 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 7 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 7 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
